revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"
Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
Bot: wrap 2 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23124421395|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{cascade-protected template}} {{High-use}} {{COinS safe|n}} {{tsh|'}} This template leaves leading space before the apostrophe, unlike the similar {{tl|`}} (no spacing). This template is intended for use with ''[[Italic type|italic]]'' text, whereas {{tl|`}} is intended for use with non-italic text. For example: * The ''Iliad''{{'}}s vocabulary is archaic. generates: * The ''Iliad''{{'}}s vocabulary is archaic. with a tiny amount of space before the apostrophe to avoid colliding with the italic ''d'' at the end of ''Iliad''. This template is also similar to {{tl|-'}}, used to slightly space apart a leading single quote from a surrounding double quotation mark provided by a template parameter in which the content is placed, e.g. the {{para|title}} parameter of {{tl|cite web}}. The equivalent template when manually placing double quotes around a single-quoted passage is {{tl|" '}}; see also {{tl|'-}} and {{tl|' "}} for the right-hand side equivalents (parameter and manual, respectively). All of these templates use 0.2 em of spacing, while this template, {{tl|'}}, uses only 0.1 em of spacing, as does {{tl|'s}}, which is used for possessives in italics. ==See also== {{Quotation mark templates}}{{Sandbox other| | }} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "params": {}, "description": "This template creates an apostrophe with a small leading space. This template is intended for use just following bold or italic text. No parameters." } [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Quotation mark templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

{{Documentation subpage}} {{cascade-protected template}} {{High-use}} {{COinS safe|n}} {{tsh|'}} This template leaves leading space before the apostrophe, unlike the similar {{tl|`}} (no spacing). This template is intended for use with ''[[Italic type|italic]]'' text, whereas {{tl|`}} is intended for use with non-italic text. For example: * The ''Iliad''{{'}}s vocabulary is archaic. generates: * The ''Iliad''{{'}}s vocabulary is archaic. with a tiny amount of space before the apostrophe to avoid colliding with the italic ''d'' at the end of ''Iliad''. This template is also similar to {{tl|-'}}, used to slightly space apart a leading single quote from a surrounding double quotation mark provided by a template parameter in which the content is placed, e.g. the {{para|title}} parameter of {{tl|cite web}}. The equivalent template when manually placing double quotes around a single-quoted passage is {{tl|" '}}; see also {{tl|'-}} and {{tl|' "}} for the right-hand side equivalents (parameter and manual, respectively). All of these templates use 0.2 em of spacing, while this template, {{tl|'}}, uses only 0.1 em of spacing, as does {{tl|'s}}, which is used for possessives in italics. ==See also== {{Quotation mark templates}}{{Sandbox other| | }} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "params": {}, "description": "This template creates an apostrophe with a small leading space. This template is intended for use just following bold or italic text. No parameters." } [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Quotation mark templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Apostrophe%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{cascade-protected template}} {{High-use}} {{COinS safe|n}} {{tsh|'}} This template leaves leading space before the apostrophe, unlike the similar {{tl|`}} (no spacing). This template is intended for use with ''[[Italic type|italic]]'' text, whereas {{tl|`}} is intended for use with non-italic text. For example: * The ''Iliad''{{'}}s vocabulary is archaic. generates: * The ''Iliad''{{'}}s vocabulary is archaic. with a tiny amount of space before the apostrophe to avoid colliding with the italic ''d'' at the end of ''Iliad''. This template is also similar to {{tl|-'}}, used to slightly space apart a leading single quote from a surrounding double quotation mark provided by a template parameter in which the content is placed, e.g. the {{para|title}} parameter of {{tl|cite web}}. The equivalent template when manually placing double quotes around a single-quoted passage is {{tl|" '}}; see also {{tl|'-}} and {{tl|' "}} for the right-hand side equivalents (parameter and manual, respectively). All of these templates use 0.2 em of spacing, while this template, {{tl|'}}, uses only 0.1 em of spacing, as does {{tl|'s}}, which is used for possessives in italics. ==See also== {{Quotation mark templates}}{{Sandbox other| | }} ==TemplateData== {{TemplateData header}} { "params": {}, "description": "This template creates an apostrophe with a small leading space. This template is intended for use just following bold or italic text. No parameters." } [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Quotation mark templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
